risk_registry.risk: OCR extraction quality may be inconsistent across international receipts.
risk_registry.mitigation: Keep manual correction mandatory in the receipt flow and preserve original source values.

risk_registry.risk: Users may not trust organizer-controlled pooled funds without strong transparency cues.
risk_registry.mitigation: Validate public contribution page copy, ledger transparency, and payout explanation with organizers and supporters.

risk_registry.risk: Trip contributions and organizer payouts may trigger processor, refund, KYC, compliance, or restricted business requirements if Crewly owns money movement.
risk_registry.mitigation: MVP uses an external crowdfunding/payment provider for checkout, KYC, refunds, disputes, and organizer payout. Crewly stores contribution references/status only and does not process, hold, refund, dispute, or pay out funds directly.

risk_registry.risk: Users may confuse trip-wide pooled funds with private event payment tracking.
risk_registry.mitigation: Stage 3 and Stage 4 must keep trip and event ledgers separate in data model, navigation, labels, summaries, and permissions.

risk_registry.risk: Public trip pages, photos, and updates may leak private information or require moderation intervention.
risk_registry.mitigation: Default media and detailed updates to private; require organizer promotion for public highlights; include reporting and audit trails.

risk_registry.risk: Translated receipt text or display-only currency conversion may be mistaken for authoritative financial truth.
risk_registry.mitigation: Display original values beside translated and converted values; label converted values as display-only.

resolved_unknowns.description: Payment processor, compliance, refund, and KYC fit for organizer-controlled pooled trip support is resolved for MVP by external-provider delegation.
resolved_unknowns.next_action: Stage 3 should define provider-agnostic integration boundaries; Stage 5 should select the specific external provider before implementation.
